figures.caption: Table 1: CMKL on continual biomedical relationship prediction (10 tasks, 5 seeds, DistMult). AP: filtered MRR over 129K+ entities; baselines share the DistMult decoder for fair comparison.

Figure 2: Per-task peak MRR for CMKL vs. single-modality ablations. Text-only excels on text-rich tasks (Disease-r1, Phenotype-r3); structural features drive other tasks; greedy-modality dynamics analyzed in Section 4.6.
Figure 4: Per-task forgetting (peak − final MRR) by modality. Red: positive; green: backward transfer. Structural encoder dominates forgetting; frozen text is inherently stable.
Figure 3: Fusion-strategy comparison. Embedding-level (MoE, gated, concat) outperforms scorelevel. Dashed lines: single-modality references.

Figure 1: Architecture of CMKL. Three modality-specific encoders produce structural (hs) via a 2-layer R-GCN, textual (ht) via frozen BiomedBERT with an optional LoRA adapter, and molecular (hm) via a Morgan-fingerprint MLP. A Mixture-of-Experts router adaptively combines them per entity via learned softmax weights (ws, wt, wm). The fused embedding hfused is scored by a DistMult decoder. Continual-learning mechanisms (bottom): modality-aware EWC applies per-modality Fisher regularization (λs, λt, λm, λf ) and K-means multimodal memory replay selects diverse exemplars from past tasks into a size-1000 buffer. The total training loss combines task, EWC, and replay terms.
Table 4: Continual learning component ablation (5 seeds, MoE-DistMult). (a) EWC strategy. (b) λ sensitivity (per-encoder, scaled by a common factor relative to default). (c) Replay buffer size.
